title: DPIA Thresholds & Triggers
description: Criteria for determining when a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) is mandatory, based on JPDP PCP No. 1/2025.

mandate.primary: Processing operations LIKELY TO RESULT IN A HIGH RISK to the protection of personal data.

quantitative_thresholds.rationale: High impact due to data sensitivity
High impact due to lack of human intervention

qualitative_factors.description: Noticeable impact on individuals' legal status/rights or their financial status, health, reputation, or access to services.
Publicly accessible areas (e.g., CCTV with profiling capability).
Use of fingerprint, face recognition, or other emerging technologies for enhanced access control/processing.
Using automated decision-making to approve or reject loan/insurance applications or other rights.
Eye-tracking, location tracking, or generating data to provide tailored direct marketing.
Processing data of children or other vulnerable subjects to offer services.

decision_logic: DPIA is REQUIRED if (a) any Quantitative Threshold is met OR (b) based on the DPO's best judgment, any Qualitative Factor suggests high risk.
